title: The Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe

notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 1, Book 5 source file.

book.title: The Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe
book.description: The Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

themes: The Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe

choiceGuidance.reason: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start

weeks.title: The Old Woman Who Lived in a Shoe Book Card
vocabulary: 1. barely - only just; almost not at all
2. worried - feeling troubled or anxious about something
1. organize - to put things in order and arrange them neatly
1. curious - wanting to know or learn about something
2. touched - feeling grateful and moved by kindness
1. laughter - the sound people make when they are happy and laughing
journal: Write a plan that could help the old woman organize a busy day.
Describe how the children can show responsibility at home.
writing: Write a plan that could help the old woman organize a busy day.
Describe how the children can show responsibility at home.
questions: 1. How many children did the old woman have?
2. What was the problem with their house?
1. What was the giant shoe made of?
2. What did the old woman find when she looked inside the shoe?
1. How did the children feel about their new home?
2. What could each child have in the new house?
1. Who helped make the shoe into a home?
2. What did the old woman plant outside?
1. Why did people come to see the shoe house?
2. How did the neighbors help the family?
1. How did the old woman feel in her new home?
2. What did the family learn about homes?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
